name: esp32_dev_board_enclosure_retro_futurist
description: Retro-futurist enclosure for a generic ESP32 development board with a hybrid-color A1-friendly lid, functional top vents, and equipment-style wall detailing.

closure.notes: The structural lid prints as a flat shell, small color inserts can print in-place with the lid, and the vent shroud remains a separate side-by-side accent part.

requirements: Preserve the same generic ESP32 dev-board fit as the plain enclosure variant.
Make the exterior feel retro-futurist with recessed panels and control-console cues.
Keep the base and structural lid printable without supports.
Provide a hybrid second-color strategy: small in-place lid inserts plus a separate side-by-side vent shroud accent.
Retain a USB cable opening on one short wall.

cutouts.name: usb_opening
cutouts.notes: Centered USB opening with a recessed service-frame surround.

notes: The custom script keeps the same internal board envelope and support strategy as the simpler enclosure.
The lid vents are real through-openings.
Badge, dial, and bolt details are modeled as flush in-place color inserts for same-object multi-color printing.
The vent shroud is a separate side-by-side accent part.
Exterior styling is Fallout-inspired without copying any specific in-world asset one-to-one.
